meanings.definition: hold back or set aside, especially for future use or contingency
examples: they held back their applause in anticipation

meanings.definition: give or assign a resource to a particular person or cause
examples: I will earmark this money for your research
She sets aside time for meditation every day

meanings.definition: obtain or arrange (for oneself) in advance
examples: We managed to reserve a table at Maxim's

meanings.definition: arrange for and reserve (something for someone else) in advance
examples: reserve me a seat on a flight
The agent booked tickets to the show for the whole family
please hold a table at Maxim's

meanings.definition: set aside for the use of a particular person or party
marked by self-restraint and reticence; -Victoria Sackville-West
examples: was habitually reserved in speech, withholding her opinion
